structData.title: Frequency cap configuration — campaign vs line item scope
structData.content:
Frequency caps control how many times a single user sees your ad within a time period. Misconfigured frequency caps are a common cause of small audiences being exhausted quickly, budgets burning too fast, or campaigns underdelivering.

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getCampaignFrequencySettings and check:
  campaignLevelCapEnabled  — whether a cap exists at campaign level
  campaignLevelCap         — the per-user limit at campaign level
  lineItemCap              — the per-user limit at each line item
  effectiveMaxPerUser      — the actual maximum impressions per user across all line items

THE ADDITIVE CAP PROBLEM
Campaign-level caps and line-item-level caps are ADDITIVE, not hierarchical. They do not override each other.

Example:
  Campaign cap: 5 per day
  4 line items, each capped at 5 per day
  Effective max per user: 20 per day (4 × 5)

The campaign cap of 5 does NOT limit the total — it adds another 5 on top of each line item's cap. This is counterintuitive and causes:
  - Users seeing far more ads than intended
  - Small audiences being exhausted rapidly
  - Budget burning faster than expected

RECOMMENDED CONFIGURATION
Option A — Line item caps only (recommended):
  Remove the campaign-level cap entirely.
  Set each line item to 2–3 impressions per user per day.
  Total per user = 2–3 per line item, controlled per placement.

Option B — Campaign cap only:
  Remove all line item caps.
  Set campaign cap to your true desired maximum (e.g. 10 per user per day across all placements).
  Simpler to manage, less granular control.

CROSS-DEVICE FREQUENCY CAPPING
Standard frequency caps apply per cookie/device ID. If a user has multiple devices (phone + laptop + tablet), they may see your ad 5 times on each device = 15 times total.
Fix: Enable cross-device frequency capping in your DSP. This requires identity resolution (deterministic matching via logged-in user IDs, or probabilistic graph). Check crossDeviceCapEnabled field.

FREQUENCY AND REACH TRADE-OFF
Lower frequency cap = more unique users reached at lower repetition.
Higher frequency cap = same users reached more often, fewer unique users.
For brand awareness: cap at 3 per user per week.
For retargeting/conversion: cap at 5–7 per user per week to stay top of mind.
